question: Is intraoperative hypotension associated with postoperative acute kidney injury?
primary_analysis: Adjusted association between exposure and outcome; model family chosen by outcome type.
models: Sensitivity analysis restricted to the arterial-line subset
limitations: Mean Arterial Pressure (MAP): Invasive MBP needs an arterial line (~1-2 s); non-invasive MBP is intermittent (every few min). Choose the source matching your hypotension definition.

Postoperative creatinine (for AKI): Present within the +/-90-day lab window BUT only when creatinine was ordered postoperatively. Many cases lack postop draws, so an AKI cohort is a SELECTED subset, not all 6,388.

plots.title: Distribution of intraoperative hypotension
plots.rationale: Inspect the exposure distribution and pick clinically sensible cut-points.

plots.title: postoperative AKI across intraoperative hypotension
plots.rationale: Show the exposure–outcome relationship before modelling.

plots.title: intraoperative hypotension by ASA
plots.rationale: Check confounding by baseline risk (ASA) across the cohort.
